name: HPE Aruba Networking Central API
what_it_does: HPE Aruba Networking Central REST API for cloud-managed wireless, wired, and SD-WAN campus networking platform. Enables AI agents to manage access point and switch configuration and deployment, handle network topology and device inventory management, access wireless client health and connectivity analytics, retrieve security alerts and threat detection events, manage WLAN and SSID policy configuration, handle guest access and captive portal management, access network performance and bandwidth analytics, retrieve IoT device visibility and segmentation data, manage SD-WAN policy and WAN performance monitoring, and integrate Aruba network data with ITSM, SIEM, and network operations platforms.
use_cases: Managing campus network device configuration from network operations agents
Retrieving wireless client health from IT helpdesk automation agents
Monitoring network performance from NOC operations agents
Integrating network alerts with ITSM from service management agents
not_for: Data center networking without campus WiFi and SD-WAN focus
Consumer home WiFi without enterprise multi-site management
Firewall and perimeter security without wireless network management
best_when: An enterprise using HPE Aruba Central wants AI agents to automate network device management, wireless performance monitoring, client troubleshooting, security alert processing, and ITSM integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated AP or switch configuration changes can cause network outages for connected users — test configuration changes in staging; bulk policy pushes require change management. Automated WLAN disable operations can impact business-critical wireless services.

auth.notes: Aruba Central uses OAuth 2.0 with access token + refresh token lifecycle. Developer portal for application registration. Python SDK (pycentral) for automation. Webhooks for network event notifications. REST API v2 with OpenAPI documentation. Ansible collection available. Rate limits vary by endpoint type.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-device annual subscription via Aruba Central
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-AP/switch/gateway subscription; Foundation and Advanced tiers
pricing.notes: San Jose, California. Aruba Networks acquired by HP (2015, $3B). Now HPE Aruba Networking. Campus networking market leader. 100,000+ customers. Strong higher education, healthcare, and retail verticals. ArubaOS-CX for switching. Competes with Cisco Meraki and Juniper Mist for cloud-managed campus networking.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth application; varies by endpoint; read vs write limits differ

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Configuration changes push to live devices immediately — always test on non-production APs/switches before bulk deployment
Token refresh lifecycle — access tokens expire; implement refresh token rotation; tokens can have short TTL
pycentral SDK available — HPE provides Python SDK (pycentral) that abstracts token management and common operations
Webhook event types vary by Central tier — some event notifications require Advanced subscription tier
Group vs device-level config — Aruba Central uses group-based config with template inheritance; understand hierarchy before automating
Rate limit variability — read endpoints vs write endpoints have different rate limits; batch operations have separate limits
agent_readiness.security_notes: Campus wireless and wired networking. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Network topology, device config, and client data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Campus networking. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US/EU. Network device config and client connectivity data.
